message:

+{
+  "version": "kc-mission-v1",
+  "name": "backstage-7684-rfc-ability-to-use-the-signed-in-users-oauth-credentials-in-the-s",
+  "missionClass": "solution",
+  "author": "KubeStellar Bot",
+  "authorGithub": "kubestellar",
+  "mission": {
+    "title": "backstage: [RFC] Ability to use the signed in users oauth credentials in the Scaffolder",
+    "description": "[RFC] Ability to use the signed in users oauth credentials in the Scaffolder. Requested by 13+ users.",
+    "type": "feature",
+    "status": "completed",
+    "steps": [
+      {
+        "title": "Check current backstage setup",
+        "description": "Verify your backstage version and configuration:\n```bash\nnpx backstage-cli info\n```\nThis feature requires a working backstage installation."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Review backstage configuration",
+        "description": "Review the relevant backstage configuration:\n**Status:** Open for comments\n\n## Need\nUsing credentials from a Personal Access Token that is a god token, or Github Apps is some times not enough security and Integrators would like to have the ability to grab a token from the users signed in oauth"
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Apply the fix for [RFC] Ability to use the signed in users oauth credentials…",
+        "description": "## Hey, I just made a Pull Request!\n\nReason for the change: we want to rely on the user's permission which is more granular and traceable compared to using the GitHub app.\n\nWe are introducing a change to the Scaffolder, we want to use the GitHub personal access token to create and push to the new repository. \n\nThe old behaviour would use the GitHub app credentials and the \"Scaffolder\" author, with this change we would check if the GitHub OAuth is enabled and prompt the user to Login with\n\nSee the source issue for community-verified solutions."
+      },
+      {
+        "title": "Verify the feature works",
+        "description": "Test that the new capability is working as expected.\nConfirm the feature described in \"[RFC] Ability to use the signed in users oauth credentials…\" is functioning correctly."
+      }
+    ],
+    "resolution": {
+      "summary": "## Hey, I just made a Pull Request!\n\nReason for the change: we want to rely on the user's permission which is more granular and traceable compared to using the GitHub app.\n\nWe are introducing a change to the Scaffolder, we want to use the GitHub personal access token to create and push to the new repository.",

+    }
